followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I keep the major-flaw verdict and essentially the same score. The other review confirms the HREI R^2 rotation-curve contradiction and adds useful concerns about the disconnection between HREI and lambda, circular calibration of alpha and T_eff, observer dependence of S, and possible degeneracy of the lambda fit. These strengthen rather than soften my prior assessment.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: I do not accept the statement that conservation follows mechanically from the Bianchi identity for the displayed T_mn; Bianchi conserves the Einstein tensor, and the displayed improvement term is not identically divergence-free by itself.
I also reject the suggestion that the slow-roll dust algebra is already plausible from the paper text; the canonical scalar pressure problem remains unresolved without an explicit derivation.
Calling the coarse-graining derivation partially supported is too generous in my view, because the microscopic operator, measure, and heat-kernel coefficients are not specified.
I treat the HREI rotation-curve contradiction as verdict-relevant, not merely a local issue, because it appears in the abstract-level phenomenological claims and conclusion.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The HREI M(R) proportional to R^2 scaling is incompatible with flat rotation curves and conflicts with the later Section 8 isothermal profile.
Alpha and T_eff are fitted to a target halo mass and are not derived from lambda, undermining the single-parameter and no-fine-tuning claims.
The observer/subsystem dependence of von Neumann entropy is a real missing foundational assumption for treating S(x) as a covariant scalar.
The reported lambda constraint may be only a reparameterized constraint on the CDM density unless the mapping and likelihood dependence are demonstrated.
The sound-speed c_s = 0 result needs a full perturbation calculation for the displayed stress tensor.
The T_eff = 3 K motivation using galaxy-scale Unruh temperatures is physically suspect.

main_claims.claim: A Planck-cell Hilbert-space lattice coarse-grains to a continuum entropy scalar S with a single dimensionless coupling lambda multiplying a canonical gradient term.
main_claims.notes: The paper gives a schematic heat-kernel/block-spin story but does not specify the microscopic action, measure, link operator, projection map, or Seeley-DeWitt coefficients needed to derive the continuum operator content.

main_claims.claim: The QMM stress-energy tensor is conserved, ghost-free, and follows from the macroscopic action.
main_claims.notes: The visible action is described as a canonical gradient action, while the stated tensor includes second-derivative improvement terms. Ghost-freedom of a quadratic fluctuation action is asserted only after adopting a particular effective form, not derived from the full system.

main_claims.claim: Under dot(S)^2 << H|dot(S)|, the QMM component behaves as pressureless cold dark matter at background and perturbative levels.
main_claims.notes: A canonical homogeneous scalar has stiff kinetic stress, not dust. The paper does not show that the improvement terms, source law, and slow-roll condition imply p approximately 0, rho proportional to a^-3, and sound speed c_s=0.

main_claims.claim: The HREI entropy-surface model naturally produces realistic halo masses and flat rotation curves.
main_claims.notes: The Section 3 model is calibrated to a target Milky-Way halo mass. Its stated M(R) proportional to R^2 gives v_c^2 proportional to R and density proportional to 1/R, while Section 8 later needs density proportional to 1/R^2 for flat curves.

main_claims.claim: Modified CLASS/MCMC analyses fit Planck 2018, BAO, and SN data essentially as well as LambdaCDM and constrain lambda to an order-unity window.
main_claims.notes: No code, likelihood files, chains, convergence diagnostics, parameter table, or reproducible spectra are supplied. If QMM exactly mimics CDM, it is also unclear whether lambda is independently constrained rather than just mapped to the fitted dark-matter density.

main_claims.claim: Pilot N-body simulations, SPARC fits, and cluster-lensing calculations reproduce halo phenomenology and reduce small-scale CDM tensions.
main_claims.notes: The simulation and fitting claims are potentially checkable but not auditable from the paper text. The N-body description appears to use CDM particles or at least collisionless numerical particles, whose physical role is not clearly separated from the claimed particle-free model.

main_claims.claim: The framework is single-parameter and non-fine-tuned.
main_claims.notes: Besides lambda, the paper introduces an entropy flux constant, an effective imprinting temperature, integration/saturation assumptions, source/write-rate parameters, and a running-coupling coefficient, without deriving them from lambda.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A precise microscopic action, measure, link matrix, and continuum limit for the Planck-cell write maps.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without these ingredients, the heat-kernel derivation of the scalar action and the exclusion of other operators are not auditable.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The entropy field S(x) is a well-defined Lorentz scalar independent of observer, slicing, and subsystem factorization.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Von Neumann entropy depends on the chosen reduced density matrix and Hilbert-space partition. The entire model assumes this can be promoted to a covariant scalar field.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The exact macroscopic action includes whatever nonminimal or boundary terms are needed to generate the displayed stress tensor.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: This determines the correct field equations, conservation law, degrees of freedom, and energy conditions.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A covariant energy-exchange model for microscopic writes Pi and ordinary matter/radiation backreaction.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: A sourced entropy field cannot be separately conserved unless the sector supplying the source is modeled, and the slow-roll/dust behavior depends on that source.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A unique relationship between HREI parameters alpha and T_eff and the field-theory coupling lambda.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this mapping, the paper combines two independent phenomenological models while claiming one parameter.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Initial conditions and primordial perturbation spectrum for S.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: CMB anisotropies, isocurvature bounds, and structure formation depend sensitively on the early-time entropy field, which the paper acknowledges is not derived.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The physical status of the particles in Qube simulations: baryons, numerical tracers, or actual collisionless dark matter.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The interpretation of the simulations as evidence for a particle-free dark-matter alternative depends on this distinction.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Boundary conditions and regularity assumptions for halo profiles and ANEC integrals.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Second-derivative stress terms are boundary-sensitive; the sign of local and averaged energy conditions cannot be inferred from the kinetic term alone.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the complete typeset PDF include a nonminimal term, boundary term, or omitted equation in Eq. (13) that would actually generate the displayed stress tensor?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The HTML extraction strips many formulas, and resolving the action/stress-tensor mismatch requires the exact typeset equations.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: For the exact action intended by the authors, is the full QMM stress tensor covariantly conserved under the sourced S equation?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires a careful variational calculation with the source sector and cannot be certified from the visible text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the full perturbation calculation give c_s = 0 and negligible anisotropic stress for the complete tensor of Eq. (15)?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper only sketches the perturbation equations; a human mathematical spot-check should recompute the gauge-invariant pressure and sound speed.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can the claimed CLASS/MCMC and Qube N-body results be reproduced from released code, parameter files, chains, and simulation outputs?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: No executable code, likelihood chains, initial conditions, or rendered convergence table values are included in the bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are Sections 3 and 8 intended to describe different radial regimes, or is the M(R) proportional to R^2 versus rho proportional to R^-2 conflict a genuine error?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The text presents both as halo-scale QMM predictions; resolving intent requires either author clarification or the complete formulas.

novelty_assessment: The information-memory language is distinctive, but the mathematical structure resembles scalar-field or emergent-gravity dark-sector phenomenology. The claimed novelty would lie in a derived QMM-to-cosmology bridge and reproducible CLASS/N-body implementations. In the supplied text, that bridge is mostly asserted, and the phenomenological halo model is calibrated and internally inconsistent, so the effective novelty is moderate at best.
score_rationale: The paper targets an important problem and has a distinctive information-theoretic framing, but the central derivations and phenomenology are not reliable in the supplied text. The action/stress-tensor mismatch, unproved conservation, unsupported dust and sound-speed limits, HREI rotation-curve contradiction, extra calibrated parameters, and non-reproducible numerical claims keep technical soundness very low. Other reviewers strengthened several objections but did not provide a reason to raise the verdict.
summary: After reading the other review, I keep my cycle-1 judgment: the paper is not technically reliable in its present form. The strongest issues are still internal rather than merely missing detail: the displayed stress tensor does not follow from the advertised action, the conservation and dust-limit arguments are not established, and the HREI halo scaling M(R) proportional to R^2 contradicts the claimed flat rotation curves. The other review usefully sharpened the parameter-count, observer-dependence, and lambda-degeneracy concerns, but I do not accept its more generous reading of conservation or slow-roll dust behavior.

technical_objections.evidence: A canonical lambda (partial S)^2 term gives the usual kinetic scalar tensor. The displayed tensor also contains g_mn Box S - nabla_m nabla_n S, which normally requires a nonminimal curvature/improvement term or a different variational principle. The paper advertises the leading operator as only the canonical gradient term.
technical_objections.objection: The stated stress tensor does not follow from the stated canonical-gradient action.

technical_objections.evidence: The divergence of g_mn Box S - nabla_m nabla_n S is not identically zero in curved spacetime; commutators generate curvature-gradient terms. The sourced equation Box S = Pi also implies energy-momentum exchange with the write sector unless that sector is specified covariantly.
technical_objections.objection: The conservation claim for T_QMM is not established and appears false for the displayed tensor without additional equations or compensating sectors.

technical_objections.evidence: For a homogeneous canonical scalar without a potential, p equals rho. Improvement terms can alter the effective pressure, but the paper does not derive p approximately 0 or rho proportional to a^-3 from dot(S)^2 << H|dot(S)| and a sparse write rate.
technical_objections.objection: The asserted pressureless-dust limit does not follow from the slow-roll condition as presented.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states cumulative mass scales as M(R) proportional to R^2. Then v_c^2 = G M(R)/R is proportional to R, not constant, and rho is proportional to 1/R rather than the isothermal 1/R^2 profile. Section 8 derives a different profile, but the paper never reconciles the two.
technical_objections.objection: The HREI radial scaling contradicts the claimed rotation-curve behavior.

technical_objections.evidence: The field theory is parameterized by lambda, while HREI introduces alpha, T_eff, and a Hubble-time accumulation prescription. Alpha is fixed by matching M = 10^12 solar masses at R = 200 kpc, and no relation to lambda is derived.
technical_objections.objection: The HREI parameterization is disconnected from the effective field theory and is circularly calibrated.

technical_objections.evidence: A canonical scalar would normally propagate with c_s^2 = 1. The paper claims negligible pressure and anisotropic stress after introducing second-derivative tensor terms, but does not show the full gauge-invariant perturbation calculation needed to justify CDM-like clustering.
technical_objections.objection: The perturbative sound speed c_s = 0 is asserted rather than derived for the full displayed stress tensor.

technical_objections.evidence: The Qube solver is described as depositing particle masses on a standard density mesh and using CDM particles with matching S-cells. If these are physical collisionless particles, the simulation does not replace dark matter; if they are tracers, their stress-energy role must be defined. Code, initial conditions, and visible convergence numbers are absent.
technical_objections.objection: The numerical evidence is not reproducible from the paper and may not test the stated no-particle alternative.

technical_objections.evidence: For the full tensor, T_mn k^m k^n includes -lambda k^m k^n nabla_m nabla_n S in addition to the positive kinetic square. The paper reduces the NEC to the kinetic term and asserts ANEC without boundary conditions or an explicit integral proof for this tensor.
technical_objections.objection: The energy-condition proof drops or inadequately controls the second-derivative part of the stress tensor.

technical_objections.evidence: The other review correctly noted that the Unruh temperature for galaxy-scale accelerations near 10^-10 m/s^2 is of order 10^-30 to 10^-31 K, not near 3 K. The chosen temperature is therefore a phenomenological fit scale, not a derived value between relevant gravitational temperatures.
technical_objections.objection: The physical motivation for T_eff = 3 K is not credible as stated.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says QMM exactly mimics pressureless dust in the relevant regime. If so, current background and linear CMB data constrain the effective matter density, and the mapping from that density to lambda must be shown before Eq. (22) can be interpreted as a genuine bound on a new coupling.
technical_objections.objection: The lambda constraint from CMB/BAO/SN may be a reparameterized CDM-density fit rather than an independent test.
